title: Avsnitt 585 - Årets miss
summary_md:
# Avsnitt 585 - Årets miss

**Feed**: fill-or-kill  
**Published**: 2026-06-16  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

Episode 585 covers a momentum-driven market: Nasdaq up ~7% on the week, a frenzied low-float SpaceX IPO, a reviving IPO market, and an Iran ceasefire/MOU that pushed oil to multi-month lows. The hosts emphasize value-unlock and spin-off plays (Vestum's water-unit sale, Volati/Salix split, Honeywell), caution against late-stage 'mania' and AI fear-mongering after Anthropic's frontier model was forced offline, and flag a possible rotation out of crowded mega-caps into value, gold and other hard assets. The closing interview with Stendörren's CEO highlights solid operational growth in logistics/light-industry real estate despite a weak share price.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 VESTUM — Vestum | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Sold Flow water business for 6.5bn, paying 13.50/sh special dividend vs ~12.50 pre-share price; remaining business valued at only ~2.50, suggesting market hasn't priced full value.

#### Reasoning

Classic value-unlock by a proven, aligned founder (Conny Ryk) with large skin in the game who previously sold to Nordic Capital. The Flow divestment realizes value far above where the market valued the whole, and a strategic review signals more action within 6-12 months. Hosts caution value cases can take time to play out.

#### Sell Triggers

- if management fails to act on remaining strategic review
- if value realization drags on for years without catalysts
- if remaining business deteriorates

> *"han lyckades sälja det här för 6,5 miljarder och ska dela ut 13,5 per aktie, kronor då. Och aktien stod i 12,5 innan."*

---

### 🟢 HON — Honeywell | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Owned ~1 year; spinning off Aerospace June 29 with Elliott as activist; host plans to sell the Aerospace spin and keep the core, also benefits from Quantinuum IPO stake.

#### Reasoning

A streamlining 'boomer' conglomerate following the GE playbook, with Elliott pushing value creation. Spin-offs historically generate excess returns (host has a dedicated spin-off focus). Host intends to keep the core business and sell the hot Aerospace unit post-spin, plus optionality from the Quantinuum quantum-computing stake.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the spin-off fails to unlock value
- if Elliott exits its activist position
- if core business margins deteriorate post-separation

> *"Jag äger ett bolag på det här temat och det är Honeywell i USA. De har ju en spin-off nu i 29 juni"*

---

### 🟢 QCOM — Qualcomm | **BUY** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Host is long ('Holcom'), sold part too early; still sees value in its chips and the on-device/edge-computing theme as AI inference moves to phones and PCs.

#### Reasoning

The forced shutdown of Anthropic's model reinforces a thesis that states and companies want controllable, local AI, favoring open-source models running on edge devices. Qualcomm's chips are positioned for this device-level compute shift; host regrets selling early and still finds it interesting.

#### Sell Triggers

- if edge/on-device AI adoption stalls
- if cloud frontier models keep dominating workloads
- if chip demand softens

> *"jag har ju långt Holcom. Den började gå upp, sålde för tidigt. Men den är fortfarande intressant tycker jag med deras chip"*

---

### 🟠 LUG — Lundin Gold | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Gold has pulled back then bounced on the Iran ceasefire; host is building a 'gold basket' and watching miners as part of a rotation into hard assets.

#### Reasoning

Hosts expect a rotation out of crowded mega-caps into value and hard assets (gold, silver, platinum) as oil falls and the Iran deal de-risks markets. Gold demand debate is two-sided (central banks may pull back to fund rearmament, but buyers like Tether are emerging). Host plans a gold basket but hasn't committed strongly.

#### Sell Triggers

- if central-bank gold buying falls sharply
- if the hard-asset rotation fails to materialize
- if real yields spike

> *"Lundin Gold och Mimera har ju rört sig väldigt mycket. Där finns det lite två spår, lite intressant."*

---

### 🟠 STEF-B — Stendörren | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Logistics/light-industry REIT delivering ~20%/yr FFO/share growth over 3 years, low ~5% vacancy, 18bn portfolio, large build-rights pipeline at ~8% yields vs ~4% funding; share price has lagged.

#### Reasoning

Strong operational momentum (overskottsgrad up to ~75-80%, expansion into Helsinki and Copenhagen) and an attractive in-house development pipeline with a wide yield-to-funding spread. Hosts note bond market and equity market diverge, with the business delivering 'under the surface' despite a weak share — a potential mispricing.

#### Sell Triggers

- if interest rates/funding costs rise sharply
- if logistics vacancies climb from current lows
- if build-rights projects fail to find tenants

> *"förvaltningsresultatet per aktie har stigit med ungefär 20% per år under de senaste tre åren"*

---
